channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli military renews orders for Palestinians to leave northern Gaza
item.description: The Israeli military on Saturday renewed its orders for Palestinian in the northern Gaza Strip to leave their homes and shelters as troops press on a weeklong offensive against militants.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Menendez brothers murder trial was 'classic battle of the sexes,' juror says
item.description: Hazel Thornton, who served as a juror during Erik and Lyle Menendez's trial, said that the men on the jury weren't swayed by claims that their father abused the brothers sexually.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What's behind the northern lights that dazzled the sky farther south than normal
item.description: Another in a series of unusually strong solar storms hitting Earth produced stunning skies full of pinks, purples, greens and blues farther south than normal, including into parts of Germany, the United Kingdom, New England and New York City.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Missing Texas mother: Suzanne Simpson's neighbor witnessed fight
item.description: Simpson, a mother of four and real estate agent, disappeared after an alleged fight with her husband Brad Simpson, 53.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Data shows migrants aren't taking 'Black jobs' or 'Hispanic jobs,' despite what Trump says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump promises the biggest deportation event the U.S. has ever seen if he is elected — a promise he has predicated, in part, on the notion that immigrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Far from where Hurricane Milton hit, tornadoes wrought unexpected damage
item.description: WELLINGTON, Fla. (AP) — Tony Brazzale, a diving boat captain who has lived for 10 years in his Wellington home in southeastern Florida, wasn't worried about Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ted Cruz and Colin Allred wage another big US Senate fight in Texas
item.description: FORT WORTH, Texas (AP) — Attack ads on every TV break. Campaign money pouring in. And on a sunny Saturday, a crowd stretching out the door for a campaign rally at Tulip's, a popular Fort Worth nightclub — this time for Democratic U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Floridians evacuated for Hurricane Milton after wake-up call from devastating Helene
item.description: BRANDON, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents who fled hundreds of miles to escape Hurricane Milton made slow trips home on crowded highways, weary from their long journeys and the cleanup work awaiting them but also grateful to be coming back alive.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY ONEIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know as Florida residents begin cleaning up after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Cleanup from Hurricane Milton was underway Friday as residents returned home after the storm barreled across Florida, spawning tornadoes, knocking out power to millions, damaging homes and leading to hundreds of rescues.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Elevator door was 'broken' during Colorado mine tour accident that killed 1 and trapped 12
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Investigators on Friday were trying to figure out what led to an elevator accident inside a former Colorado gold mine that killed a tour guide, injured four others and left a separate group of 12 people trapped for hours at the bottom of the tourist attraction 1,000 feet (305 meters) b
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, AMY BETH HANSON, MEAD GRUVER and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: North Carolina governor blasts 'flat out lie' from Trump on disaster aid
item.description: "Trump’s lies and conspiracy theories have hurt the morale of first responders and people who lost everything, helped scam artists and put government and rescue workers in danger," said North Carolina Gov. Roy Cooper.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Actress Jessica Chastain slams airline over $15 credit
item.description: The actress was unhappy after the seatback screen didn't work on her recent Jet Blue flight. So, she took to X, formerly Twitter, to express her dismay.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KREX 5

item.title: Biden to visit Hurricane Milton-ravaged Florida with damage estimates 'around $50 billion'
item.description: The White House announced President Biden is scheduled to visit Florida on Sunday to survey the aftermath of Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 Europeans engineered $95M Texas Lotto win with over 25M tickets
item.description: It was successful for one group last year, but Texas Lottery officials have made changes to inhibit anyone else from trying it.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Allred pushes back on Cruz transgender attack ads
item.description: “Ted Cruz is lying again, but now he's lying about our children. I'm a dad. I'm also a Christian,” Allred said on camera.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Trial on hold for New Jersey man charged in knife attack that injured Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — The attempted murder trial of the man charged with severely injuring author Salman Rushdie in a 2022 knife attack was put on hold Friday while judges consider a request to move it to another county. Jury selection had been scheduled to start on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Road rage shooting in LA leaves 1 dead, shuts down Interstate 5 for hours
item.description: An apparent road rage shooting during rush hour traffic in Los Angeles has killed one person and injured another.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Montana businessman gets 2 years in prison for role in Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the US Capitol
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A Montana business owner and supporter of former President Donald Trump has been sentenced to two years in federal prison for his role in the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol that interrupted certification of the 2020 Electoral College vote.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Experts warn 'crazy busy' Atlantic hurricane season is far from over
item.description: Millions of people in the southeastern U.S. still are reeling from the catastrophic damage caused by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, but scientists warn that the Atlantic hurricane season is far from over. “As far as hurricane landfalls in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1 killed, 23 rescued after equipment malfunction at tourist gold mine in Colorado
item.description: One person was killed and 23 were rescued after being trapped for about six hours inside a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KRON4

item.title: Family sues after finding out on TikTok funeral home sent mom's body to wrong country
item.description: A family in New York City is suing a funeral home for mistakenly sending their mother Carmen Maldonado's casket to Guatemala for burial instead of Ecuador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: reports
item.description: The Internet Archive, a popular digital library known for its Wayback Machine, was hacked and suffered a breach that reportedly exposed the data of 31 million user accounts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, ABC 4

item.title: Why do news media call the winners of elections?
item.description: It’s election night, the polls have closed and chances are you’re waiting on The Associated Press or one of the major television networks to say who will be the next president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 1 person dies and 12 are rescued after elevator malfunctions at Colorado gold mine tourist site
item.description: DENVER (AP) — One person was killed and 12 people were rescued after being trapped for about six hours at the bottom of a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikToker Morgan Roos on riding out Milton: ‘It got dicey’
item.description: Roos, a new Tampa resident, recounted spending the night in the hallway of her high-rise apartment building.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors reviewing 'new evidence' in Marilyn Manson sexual abuse probe
item.description: The musician and artist, whose real name is Brian Warner, has vehemently denied the allegations, though he has settled at least two related lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Milton by the numbers: At least 5 dead, at least 12 tornadoes, 3.4M without power
item.description: Hurricane Milton's explosive formation stirred high anxiety for Florida less than two weeks after Helene raked the state and much of the Southeast. On Thursday, authorities were still assessing the damage, even as Gov. Ron DeSantis said it fell short of “the worst-case scenario.
item.dc:creator: MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 14-year-old found floating on fence in Tampa floodwaters after Hurricane Milton
item.description: A 14-year-old had to be rescued after Hurricane Milton's floodwaters trapped him on a floating fence in the middle of Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Milton and Helene leave students, schools reeling
item.description: As Hurricane Milton slams into Florida — and amid Helene's lingering destruction — students across the southeast are facing severe disruptions to their academic year, posing challenges for both educators and children after they have already witnessed devastation in their communities.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: More than 2 million without power as Hurricane Milton slams Florida, causes deaths and flooding
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Hurricane Milton plowed into Florida as a Category 3 storm Wednesday, bringing misery to a coast still ravaged by Helene, pounding cities with winds of over 100 mph (160 kph) after producing a barrage of tornadoes, but sparing Tampa a direct hit.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tropical dictionary: Words to know as active hurricane season continues
item.description: Let’s dive into some of the frequently used words and phrases that are on repeat during this busy week of hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: Alex Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: COVID may increase risk of heart attack, death years after infection: study
item.description: COVID-19 could increase the risk of major cardiac events, like strokes and heart attacks, or death from any cause, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Jenna Fischer of 'The Office' reveals breast cancer diagnosis, recovery
item.description: Jenna Fischer, the actress well-known for her role as Pam on "The Office," revealed her diagnosis and recovery in a post to social media.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: State police recruit's death in Massachusetts overshadows graduation ceremony
item.description: WORCESTER, Mass. (AP) — Holding signs demanding truth and chanting for justice, several dozen people gathered Wednesday outside a graduation ceremony for Massachusetts State Police cadets demanding an explanation of how one of the recruits died during a training exercise.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Will we see northern lights, power grid impacts amid 'severe' geomagnetic storm?
item.description: Earth is being hit by a strong solar radiation storm event that will be followed by a geomagnetic storm, space weather officials say.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, widow of Robert F. Kennedy, suffers stroke
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The family of Ethel Kennedy, the widow of Robert F. Kennedy, says she's been hospitalized after suffering a stroke.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform is addictive, harms kids' mental health
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia sued TikTok on Tuesday, alleging the platform exploits and harms young users while "deceiving" the public about these dangers.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4
